markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Map every WP-009 feature, primitive, tool, UI, storage surface, and validator lane to applicable HBR rows and build_rules constraints. This MT belongs to ResearchSpecTranslation. Convert research, operator intent, and current authority into self-contained WP/spec-ready requirements.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Research papers and GitHub/codebase findings are context inputs only; translate every accepted idea into Handshake-native requirements before activation.
Activation must cite SPEC_CURRENT and the v02.192 indexed bundle as current authority, not the v02.182 monolith alone.
scope.expected_behavior: BuildRulesApplicabilityMap leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: BuildRulesApplicabilityMap is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Per-surface HBR applicability matrix recorded as packet.json acceptance_matrix.hbr (13 typed rows keyed by feature/primitive/tool/UI/storage/lane surface, each with applicable HBR rule ids, owner MT range, required flag, evidence_kind).
Pillar tags INT/SWARM/VIS/QUIET/MAN/STOP declared in packet.json hbr.tags_declared.
Per-MT hbr_focus already present in all 240 MT contracts (stub-promoted).

AC3 (no sidecar/outside-runtime authority): the applicability matrix lives in packet.json acceptance_matrix (typed JSON contract), not in markdown, sidecar papers, or external tooling; Actual hbr_focus rows are HBR-INT-001/HBR-INT-004/HBR-MAN-001/HBR-SWARM-001 (no CX rows are cited as substitutes). HBR registry .GOV/roles_shared/records/HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json is the rule authority.

AC4 (each hbr_focus row proven or marked NOT_APPLICABLE): MT-009 is a record-only deliverable that produces the HBR applicability matrix; per actual hbr_focus = [HBR-INT-001, HBR-INT-004, HBR-MAN-001, HBR-SWARM-001]: HBR-INT-001 = SATISFIED (the applicability matrix is a typed in-governance JSON contract at packet.json acceptance_matrix.hbr, no markdown/sidecar/external authority). HBR-INT-004 = NOT_APPLICABLE (this MT changes no product code, runtime behavior, or trace surface; it is a governance-record artifact). HBR-MAN-001 = NOT_APPLICABLE (no model-callable command, IPC channel, schema field, config key, or CLI flag is added/changed by this record MT, so no UserManual update is triggered). HBR-SWARM-001 = NOT_APPLICABLE (no concurrent runtime/CRDT/EventLedger behavior is introduced by this record MT). The matrix's per-surface rows carry the runtime HBR obligations for the owner MT groups; they are not this record MT's own proof obligations.
Spec 12.7 reconciliation: all 15 spec-declared HBR rules now present in the matrix (acceptance_matrix.spec_12_7_reconciliation); supplementary stricter rules retained and justified.

concerns: Contract freshness skew: scope.relevant_files and the activation constraint pin the v02.191 bundle while SPEC_CURRENT and all evidence are v02.192 (contracts hydrated 07:20Z after the 06:45Z spec bump); validator should read the constraint intent (indexed bundle, not v02.182 monolith), but a hygiene pass updating contract pins to v02.192 would remove the ambiguity.

AC coverage: evidence addresses only the matrix deliverable; AC3 (sidecar-research rejection restated) and AC4 (HBR rows proven/blocked/not-applicable for THIS MT with concrete reasons) have no evidence entries; MT-009 gives no disposition for its own hbr_focus rows (HBR-INT-001/INT-004/MAN-001/SWARM-001), unlike the other 29 reviewed MTs.

Matrix completeness vs scope: scope demands mapping every feature, primitive, tool, UI, storage surface, AND validator lane; acceptance_matrix.hbr has 13 rows keyed by implementation MT-group surface only; ResearchSpecTranslation (MT-001..016) and MasterSpecEnrichmentSeed (MT-033..048) appear in no row and acceptance_matrix.hbr_not_applicable is empty [], so two of fifteen MT groups have no explicit matrix disposition; no row is keyed by validator lane or by the thirteen 12.7 primitives.

Matrix vs spec floor: 12-end-of-file-appendices.md#12.7 declares HBR rows applicable at packet hydration including HBR-INT-003, HBR-INT-006, HBR-INT-007, HBR-SWARM-004, HBR-VIS-001, HBR-QUIET-002 - none of these six appears in any matrix row applicable list (union = INT-001/004, MAN-001/002/003, SWARM-001/002/003, QUIET-001/003, VIS-002/003, STOP, CX-503R/S).

Loose rule citation: rows cite bare HBR-STOP (registry defines HBR-STOP-001..005) and CX-503R/CX-503S which live in the Codex, not HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json (both verified to exist in Handshake_Codex_v1.4.md).
adversarial_review.refutation_attempts: Opened every cited artifact (refinement.json, spec-enrichment-proposal.json, stub contract, v02.192 spec modules 02/07/10/11/12, spec-changelog.jsonl, SPEC_CURRENT.md, packet.json); attacked criterion coverage AC1-AC4, citation validity, specificity, v02.192 freshness, and scope-artifact existence. Verified the 13 rows, row fields, hbr.tags_declared, and per-MT hbr_focus on all 240 contracts programmatically; diffed the matrix rule-ID union against the spec 12.7 applicability row and the HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES registry; cross-checked group_index for unmapped groups. Fix: add explicit not_applicable matrix rows for the two research/spec MT groups (and a validator-lane disposition), reconcile or justify the six missing 12.7 rules per row, and add AC3/AC4 evidence entries to this MT.

items: Added 6 spec-12.7-declared rules missing from matrix (HBR-INT-003/006/007, HBR-SWARM-004, HBR-VIS-001, HBR-QUIET-002) to owning surface rows.
Added hbr_not_applicable dispositions for ResearchSpecTranslation + MasterSpecEnrichmentSeed groups and external-validator lane.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.

findings.summary: The MT contract evidence contradicts its own HBR focus set: the ready evidence cites CX-503S/CX-503P while the MT hbr_focus is HBR-INT-001, HBR-INT-004, HBR-MAN-001, and HBR-SWARM-001.
findings.required_remediation: Rewrite the MT-009 handoff/evidence block so it explicitly proves or marks not-applicable each actual hbr_focus row: HBR-INT-001, HBR-INT-004, HBR-MAN-001, and HBR-SWARM-001. Do not cite unrelated CX rows as substitutes for the MT HBR rows.

evidence_basis: Authoritative MT JSON contract and current product tree inspection.
Dependency failures use deterministic packet status/dependency reads only; WIP MT work products were not inspected.
remediation_summary: Rewrite the MT-009 handoff/evidence block so it explicitly proves or marks not-applicable each actual hbr_focus row: HBR-INT-001, HBR-INT-004, HBR-MAN-001, and HBR-SWARM-001. Do not cite unrelated CX rows as substitutes for the MT HBR rows.

remediation_v2.fail_verdict: FAIL_V1 HBR_FOCUS_EVIDENCE_MISMATCH (HIGH): evidence cited CX-503S/CX-503P instead of the actual hbr_focus rows
remediation_v2.remediation: Rewrote AC4 evidence to prove/NA each actual hbr_focus row (HBR-INT-001 SATISFIED; HBR-INT-004/MAN-001/SWARM-001 NOT_APPLICABLE for this record-only MT); removed the unrelated CX-503S/CX-503P citation.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
